A.title: Donald Trump
A.description:
You are bold, instinctive, and unapologetic in how you move through the world. You don’t wait for permission, consensus, or perfect conditions. You act, adjust, and push forward with confidence. You thrive in chaotic environments where others hesitate, and you’re energized by competition, attention, and high stakes. You believe leadership means taking up space, setting the tone, and forcing momentum when things stall.

You’re at your best when boldness matters more than polish. You spot opportunities quickly and are willing to break norms if they slow you down. People may find you polarizing, but they never overlook you.

B.title: Barack Obama
B.description:
You lead through ideas, communication, and perspective. You naturally see the bigger picture and help others understand it too. People trust you because you’re thoughtful, composed under pressure, and capable of turning complexity into clarity. You believe influence comes from bringing people along, not forcing them.

You excel at shaping direction, setting culture, and aligning people around shared goals. You think long-term and care deeply about purpose and legacy. When tension rises, you don’t escalate. You elevate the conversation.

C.title: Nicolás Maduro
C.description:
You are resilient under pressure and difficult to shake. When situations become unstable, you don’t panic. You endure. You adapt. You hold your ground. You rely on inner conviction, loyalty, and persistence more than external validation. Survival itself is a form of strength for you.

You’re especially strong in environments where others would collapse or give up. You may not seek approval, but you have an exceptional ability to last, resist, and push through adversity.

D.title: Vladimir Putin
D.description:
You think strategically, quietly, and several steps ahead. You value control, leverage, and timing over visibility. You rarely act emotionally and prefer to observe, calculate, and move only when the advantage is clear. You believe real power doesn’t need to announce itself.

You’re strongest behind the scenes, shaping outcomes through positioning rather than force. Stability, influence, and long-term control matter more to you than short-term wins.

questions.text: When you walk into a room full of strangers, what do you do first?

answers.text: Take charge and make yourself known
Observe quietly and assess power dynamics
Wait and see how things unfold

questions.text: A big decision must be made quickly. You:

answers.text: Trust your instincts and act fast

questions.text: How do you respond to public criticism?

answers.text: Ignore it and stay the course
Use it to your strategic advantage

questions.text: Your leadership style is best described as:
When things start to go wrong, you:
What matters most to you as a leader?

answers.text: They are tools to be used

questions.text: In a crisis, your instinct is to:

answers.text: Depend on the few you trust

questions.text: Deep down, you believe leaders should:
